title: Business Context
tagline: Resolve business jargon to canonical dbt models and metrics
summary: The context tool performs glossary, fiscal, org, and compliance business-context search with synonym resolution, mapping fuzzy stakeholder language such as "annual revenue last fiscal year" to canonical dbt terms (ARR), the metrics that define them, and the models that materialize them. Per dbt Wizard's own description of its capabilities, context supports four kinds: glossary, fiscal, org, and compliance, so a regulated term like "PII" or a SOX-controlled balance can resolve to the governed model that satisfies it. Every match arrives with DAG-linked models attached, so the wizard can immediately introspect those nodes via describe to confirm grain, primary keys, and test state. Because the context layer indexes the same local DuckDB project graph as the rest of the toolset, it proactively surfaces which linked models lack documentation or test coverage and offers to fill those gaps in the same turn.
whyDifferent: A general AI coding agent treats "annual revenue" as a literal string to grep, returning every file where the substring happens to appear and missing models named arr, mrr, or fct_subscriptions entirely. The context tool resolves the business synonym to its canonical glossary term first, then returns the exact metric and linked models with full DAG context, not text matches. The same applies to governed language: a compliance-kind lookup maps a regulated concept (PII, SOX-controlled, GDPR-restricted) to the specific model that is supposed to satisfy that control, something a grep-based agent has no way to know. It also knows, from the live index, which of those models are undocumented or untested, so it can close coverage gaps a grep-based agent would never even see.

capabilities.title: Synonym and jargon resolution
capabilities.detail: Maps fuzzy business phrasing to canonical dbt terms: "annual revenue" -> ARR, "churn" -> logo_churn_rate, "last fiscal year" -> the org's fiscal calendar boundaries. Resolution is glossary-driven, not substring matching, so stakeholder language reaches the right model even when the phrasing never appears in the SQL.

capabilities.title: Fiscal and org calendar awareness
capabilities.detail: Understands the project's fiscal-year and period definitions so relative time language ("last fiscal year", "current quarter") resolves to concrete date boundaries tied to the canonical revenue models, not the literal calendar year. The org kind covers team ownership and domain structure for the same project graph.

capabilities.title: Compliance term resolution
capabilities.detail: The compliance kind resolves governed and regulatory language (PII, SOX-controlled, GDPR-restricted, HIPAA) to the specific dbt model designated to satisfy that control, with its classification and masking-policy context attached. Resolving a regulated term to its governed model, rather than to a substring match, is what lets the wizard reason about whether the right model is in place and whether it is documented and tested.

capabilities.title: DAG-linked results
capabilities.detail: Every context match returns the linked models and (where applicable) the MetricFlow metric, so the wizard can pivot straight into describe for grain, PK markers, materialization, and last-run test state without a second discovery step.

capabilities.title: Proactive doc and test gap surfacing
capabilities.detail: Because the context index sees the same project graph, it flags which linked models have missing descriptions or zero tests at resolution time, turning a definition lookup into an actionable coverage finding. For compliance-kind matches this doubles as a control-evidence check: a governed model with no tests is a flagged gap.

capabilities.title: Gap remediation handoff
capabilities.detail: After surfacing a documentation or test gap, the wizard offers to close it: a test_writer subagent authors the schema.yml docs and tests, then a validation subagent runs dbt_test to confirm the new tests pass.

examplePrompts.prompt: What model holds our annual revenue for last fiscal year, and is it documented?
examplePrompts.intent: Resolve the business term ARR to its canonical model and check doc/test coverage.

examplePrompts.prompt: A stakeholder asked for net revenue retention. Which metric and model is that?
examplePrompts.intent: Map NRR jargon to the canonical MetricFlow metric and linked model.

examplePrompts.prompt: Where do we define churn, and does the model behind it have tests?
examplePrompts.intent: Resolve churn to logo_churn_rate, surface the linked model, and report test gaps.

examplePrompts.prompt: Which model is our governed PII customer table, and does it have tests proving the masking holds?
examplePrompts.intent: Use a compliance-kind context lookup to resolve the regulated term PII to its governed model, then introspect for test coverage as control evidence.

examplePrompts.prompt: Resolve "active customers in the current quarter" to a model and tell me its grain.
examplePrompts.intent: Fiscal-aware resolution plus grain confirmation via introspection.

examplePrompts.prompt: The finance team says "bookings" but I can't find a model called that. What's the canonical term?
examplePrompts.intent: Synonym resolution from a business alias with no literal SQL match.

examplePrompts.prompt: Resolve "gross margin" to its metric and, if the underlying model lacks tests, draft them.
examplePrompts.intent: Resolve the term and surface the test gap on the linked model, then hand off to a test_writer subagent that authors the schema.yml tests; the validation subagent runs dbt_test afterward.

interaction.scenario: A finance stakeholder asks an SE for "annual revenue last fiscal year." The wizard resolves the jargon to ARR, finds the canonical model, and discovers it is undocumented and undertested.
interaction.userPrompt: Finance is asking for annual revenue last fiscal year. Which model is that, and is it trustworthy?

events.body: Finance is asking for annual revenue last fiscal year. Which model is that, and is it trustworthy?

"Annual revenue" is business jargon, not a model name. I'll resolve it through the business-context index with synonym expansion rather than grepping the SQL, and let it attach the fiscal boundaries and linked models.

Resolved to canonical term ARR, metric arr, and gold model fct_arr. The org fiscal year ends Jan 31, so "last fiscal year" is FY2025 = 2025-02-01 to 2026-01-31, not the calendar year. The index flags fct_arr as undocumented with zero tests. Let me introspect it to confirm grain and last-run state.

Grain is one row per customer per fiscal_period, PK (customer_id, fiscal_period). It feeds the exec_revenue_dashboard exposure but has zero tests and no docs. That is a trust gap on a board-facing number. I'll have a test_writer subagent author a uniqueness test on the compound PK, not_null on arr_usd, and column descriptions, then the validation subagent will run them.

events.code:
models:
  - name: fct_arr
    description: "Annual recurring revenue (ARR) per customer per fiscal period. Grain: one row per customer per fiscal_period. Feeds exec_revenue_dashboard."
    tests:
      - dbt_utils.unique_combination_of_columns:
          combination_of_columns: [customer_id, fiscal_period]
    columns:
      - name: customer_id
        description: "Customer identifier; FK to dim_customers."
        tests: [not_null]
      - name: fiscal_period
        description: "First day of the fiscal period this ARR snapshot covers."
        tests: [not_null]
      - name: arr_usd
        description: "Annual recurring revenue in USD for the customer in this fiscal period."
        tests: [not_null]

PASS unique_combination_of_columns_fct_arr (0 failures)
PASS not_null_fct_arr_customer_id
PASS not_null_fct_arr_fiscal_period
PASS not_null_fct_arr_arr_usd
4 of 4 tests passed

events.body: "Annual revenue last fiscal year" resolves to the canonical term ARR (metric: arr), materialized in gold model fct_arr (schema analytics_gold), grain one row per customer per fiscal_period. Last fiscal year for this org is FY2025: 2025-02-01 through 2026-01-31, since the fiscal year ends Jan 31. Trust caveat at resolution time: fct_arr had zero tests and no docs despite feeding the exec_revenue_dashboard exposure. A test_writer subagent authored a description plus a compound-PK uniqueness test and three not_null tests; the validation subagent ran them with --defer against the production manifest in target-prod/ and all 4 pass. The model is now documented and covered for the number finance is asking about.

seNotes: Lead with the jargon-to-canonical gap: type "annual revenue" and show context returns the ARR metric and fct_arr model, then point out a grep agent finds nothing because no SQL contains that phrase. That contrast is the whole demo.

Emphasize fiscal awareness. "Last fiscal year" resolving to the org's Feb 1 to Jan 31 boundaries (not the calendar year) is a detail finance audiences instantly trust and competitors rarely get right.

Show all four context kinds. Per dbt Wizard's own description, context covers glossary, fiscal, org, and compliance. The compliance kind is the one regulated-industry buyers ask about: resolve a term like PII or SOX-controlled to the governed model that is supposed to satisfy it, then check its tests as control evidence.

The gap surfacing is automatic, not a separate scan. surface_gaps is on by default, so the undocumented/untested flag comes back in the same context result, in one round trip. For a compliance-kind match, a governed model with zero tests is a control-evidence gap, not just a coverage gap.

Show the handoff. Resolving a term and then closing the gap in the same turn (a test_writer subagent authors the docs and tests, the validation subagent runs dbt_test with --defer against the production manifest in target-prod/) is what turns a lookup into governance.
Stay product-accurate: context resolves and links, describe introspects. It does not run the metric itself. If they want the actual number, that is the metrics tool, which is a different page.

tools.name: mcp__dbt_index__context
tools.purpose: Search the glossary, fiscal, org, and compliance business-context index with synonym resolution, mapping business jargon to canonical dbt terms, the metrics that define them, and the linked models that materialize them. The compliance kind resolves governed and regulatory terms (PII, SOX-controlled, GDPR-restricted) to the model designated to satisfy that control. Optionally flags doc and test coverage gaps on the linked models.

params.name: query
params.desc: The business-language phrase to resolve, e.g. "annual revenue last fiscal year" or a governed term like "customer PII".

params.name: kind
params.desc: Scope the search to a context domain: glossary (business terms and synonyms), fiscal (fiscal-year and period boundaries), org (team ownership and domain structure), or compliance (governed and regulatory terms mapped to the models that satisfy them).

params.name: resolve_synonyms
params.desc: Expand the query through the synonym graph (e.g. annual revenue -> ARR) before matching. Default true.

params.name: include_linked_models
params.desc: Attach the canonical metric and linked dbt models to each match. Default true.

params.name: surface_gaps
params.desc: Annotate linked models with missing-doc and zero-test flags; for compliance-kind matches this serves as a control-evidence check. Default true.

tools.returns: Ranked context matches, each with the canonical term, the matched synonyms, an optional MetricFlow metric, the linked models with their layer and materialization, and per-model doc/test coverage flags. Compliance-kind matches additionally carry the regulatory classification of the governed model.

tools.name: mcp__dbt_index__describe
tools.purpose: Full node introspection for a model resolved by context: columns, compiled SQL, last-run test results, lineage, catalog types, grain, PK markers, schema, and materialization.

params.name: node
params.desc: The dbt node to introspect, e.g. fct_arr or dim_customers.

params.name: include
params.desc: Which detail sections to return, e.g. ["columns","compiled_sql","tests","lineage","catalog"]. Defaults to the column list, last-run test results, and immediate lineage.

tools.returns: A structured node profile: grain, PK, materialization, schema, columns with types, test results, and lineage edges.

charts.title: Coverage gaps context surfaces
charts.note: dbt Wizard surfaces doc and test coverage gaps proactively so they get filled before an incident.
